column1: # The dependency graph for Layer 4 (inference) substrate chunks. Each
# row declares one chunk + its tier + its Hβ-infer-substrate.md §
# citation + its imports (must be defined in priors). build.sh
# assembles per this order; future drift-audit verifies imports
#   §2.3 — Ty tag conventions (ty.wat — coordinated with Hβ.lower)
# Per the eight interrogations on Hβ.infer structure (mirroring the
# runtime/INDEX.tsv discipline at the inference layer):
#   Graph?       — this file IS the dep graph for Layer 4 chunks
#   Handler?     — build.sh is the assembler-handler reading this
#   Verb?        — chunk concatenation is `|>` per row order
#   Row?         — each chunk's "uses" column declares its symbol-row
#   Ownership?   — each chunk OWNS its exports; consumers `ref` them
#   Refinement?  — Hβ-infer-substrate.md § cite IS the per-chunk
#   Gradient?    — each row added unlocks one inference capability
#   Reason?      — hβ_section + commit message records why each landed
# Tiers (dependency depth FROM the inference layer's point of view —
# Layer 4 sits atop Layer 1 runtime substrate which provides Tier 0-4
# of its own dep stack; see runtime/INDEX.tsv for that):
#   4 — depends only on Layer 1 runtime substrate (no infer-layer deps)
#   6 — depends on Tier 5 + Tier 4 + Layer 1 runtime
#   7 — depends on Tier 6 + Tier 5 + Tier 4 + Layer 1 runtime
# unify.wat / own.wat / walk_expr.wat / walk_stmt.wat / main.wat) get
# appended as their chunks land per Hβ-infer-substrate.md §13.3
# dep order. NO placeholder rows for unlanded chunks per the residue
